purpose: The commit fixes a bug in the Bluetooth HCI sync module where commands could be queued after the HCI device has been unregistered. It ensures that hci_cmd_sync_queue returns an error if the HCI_UNREGISTER flag is set, preventing a use-after-free (UAF) vulnerability that would occur when the hdev structure is freed after a timeout.

The commit removes an unnecessary include directive 'asm/termios.h' from the file n_hdlc.c. It explains that this include was redundant because 'linux/tty.h' is already included, and that pulls in 'linux/termios.h' as well. The change addresses a code organization issue and simplifies the include structure, making the code cleaner and avoiding potential confusion or complications due to redundant includes.

This commit updates documentation to reflect the addition of a new 'N/A' metric for store operations in the perf c2c (cache-to-cache) subsystem. The change involves updating the report table documentation to include this new metric, which indicates that store operations are not applicable for certain measurements. The commit does not introduce functional changes to the code itself, but rather updates documentation to align with existing or upcoming code changes.

This commit fixes issues in the hugetlb (huge page) subsystem related to vma (virtual memory area) lock handling during vma splitting and range unmapping. It addresses a race condition in hugetlb_unmap_file_folio and ensures that vma locks are properly managed during vma duplication (splitting). The commit also corrects how vma locks are used to prevent pmd (page middle directory) sharing, fixing potential double-free issues and ensuring correct synchronization.

This commit adds documentation for two power supplies (AVDD and MICVDD) used by the rt5682s codec. The changes are limited to the device tree binding file, where these supplies are documented to be used by the sc7180-trogdor-kingoftown.dtsi file. The commit does not introduce functional changes to the code, but rather updates the device tree bindings to reflect existing usage.

This commit fixes a resource leak in the SCSI Zorro7xx driver. The issue occurs during the removal of a device where an ioremap() call is not properly undone with iounmap() in the remove function. The commit adds the missing iounmap() call to ensure proper resource cleanup during error handling paths.

The commit removes an unused 'work' field from the struct mlx5e_async_ctx in the Mellanox 5 Ethernet driver. This is a straightforward cleanup change that eliminates dead code, improving code clarity and reducing memory usage.

The commit changes the data type of the 'key_idx' member in the 'cfg80211_auth_request' structure from unsigned to signed. This change is made to avoid warnings from static checkers like smatch, which occur when -1 is assigned to 'key_idx' in cases where the key is NULL. The change is minimal and focused on a specific type definition to prevent false positives in static analysis.

The commit sorts the CONFIG_SURFACE_PLATFORMS configuration option in RISC-V defconfigs. The change is described as having no functional impact, aligning the order with savedefconfig's output. It's a cosmetic change to configuration file ordering.

The commit introduces a new helper function 'mcp251xfd_is_fd_mode()' to check if the chip's FIFOs are configured for CAN-FD mode, and replaces existing open-coded checks with calls to this new function. This is a refactoring change that improves code organization and maintainability by centralizing the logic for determining CAN-FD mode.

The commit modifies a selftest for the RTC (Real-Time Clock) subsystem to skip the test when an RTC device is not present. This prevents the test suite from failing unnecessarily in environments where the RTC hardware is absent. The change involves adjusting the test logic to detect RTC availability and conditionally execute or skip the test.

The commit removes a redundant assignment to the variable 'bytes' in the ALSA echoaudio driver. The variable is assigned a value that is never read and is reassigned within a subsequent if block. This change eliminates a dead store warning from clang scan build and cleans up the code by removing unnecessary operations.

This commit modifies the kernel's RCU (Read-Copy-Update) subsystem to allow the rcuscale feature to be built without requiring RCU Tasks. In kernels with CONFIG_PREEMPT_NONE=y, RCU Tasks is normally substituted for normal RCU, but this commit decouples the two, preventing unnecessary inclusion of RCU Tasks when rcuscale is built. This reduces kernel size and simplifies tracing operations.

The commit removes a duplicated phrase 'be split' from the spte.h file in the KVM x86/mmudirectory. The change corrects a typo where 'be split be split' was incorrectly written instead of 'be split'. This is a straightforward textual correction in a header file that affects how certain memory management operations are described or documented.

This commit reverts a previous change that populated a DCN315 clock table based on DCFCLK. The reason for the revert is that when DCFCLK states are disabled, the PMFW fills out the table in a way that causes a 'no read' situation, leading to potential underflow. The revert prevents this issue until a better solution is implemented.

This commit adds device tree binding documentation for the Renesas RZ/V2L SoC's Bifrost Mali-G31 GPU. It introduces a new compatible string in the device tree bindings to enable proper hardware support for this specific GPU in the Linux kernel. The change is purely documentation-based and adds support for a new hardware platform.

The commit renames the struct pcie_port to dw_pcie_rp in the DesignWare PCIe core driver to improve naming consistency. It follows the existing convention where other driver entities use the 'dw_' prefix and endpoint-related entities use the '_ep' suffix. This is a purely cosmetic change that improves code readability and maintainability without altering functionality.

This commit fixes a Clang compiler warning related to incorrect format specifiers in a printf-like function call. Specifically, it addresses a warning where 'unsigned short' was passed to a format specifier expecting 'unsigned short' but the argument gets promoted to 'int' due to C's default argument promotion rules. The fix changes the format specifier from '%hx' to '%x' to match the promoted type, ensuring correct compilation without warnings.

This commit addresses an issue in the MIPS architecture's jump_label implementation related to a branch range check. It fixes a problem where the upper bound of the branch range was not cast to a long type before performing a signed comparison, which could lead to incorrect behavior or warnings when dealing with negative offsets. The fix ensures proper handling of signed comparisons in the branch range validation logic.

This commit removes the HW_VAR_H2C_FW_P2P_PS_OFFLOAD case from the SetHwReg8188EU() function in the r8188eu staging driver. The change involves calling the function directly instead of going through a switch-case structure. This is part of a larger effort to eliminate unnecessary HAL (Hardware Abstraction Layer) code, simplifying the driver's hardware register handling logic.

The commit addresses sparse warnings by declaring non-global symbols as static in the ARM64 kernel code. Specifically, it fixes a warning about the symbol 'cavium_erratum_23154_cpus' not being declared as static in arch/arm64/kernel/cpu_errata.c. This change ensures proper symbol linkage and resolves a compiler warning, improving code correctness and adherence to kernel coding standards.

This commit corrects an incorrect specification in device tree bindings for the MDSS (Mobile Display Subsystem) driver. Specifically, it removes bogus interrupt flags cells from MDSS consumer nodes, because the MDSS interrupt provider is a single-cell provider and does not require interrupt flags on the consumers. This is a fix for a misconfiguration in the device tree that could lead to incorrect interrupt handling or failures in display subsystem operation.

The commit modifies the V4L2 controls subsystem to allocate arrays within the struct ctrl_ref. This change prepares the codebase for allowing drivers to dynamically change the array size, which implies a refactor to support more flexible control handling. The change is technical and related to memory allocation and data structure management in the media subsystem.

This commit adds support for additional PMICs and SoCs to the socinfo driver in the Qualcomm subsystem. Specifically, it includes entries for SM8350, SC8280XP, SA8540P, and another SM8450, along with various PMICs found on boards for these platforms. The change is purely additive, extending the driver's knowledge of hardware components without altering existing behavior or fixing known issues.

This commit updates the rtl8xxxu WiFi driver to recognize all possible chip cuts (versions) from A to P (0-15) instead of only up to E. Previously, when a chip cut greater than E was encountered, the driver would print 'unknown'. This change ensures that all valid chip versions are properly identified, improving driver compatibility and correctness.

This commit removes the custom software enable count (.is_enabled state) from PRCMU clocks in the clk: ux500 driver. The rationale is that the core already maintains a software enable count, making the custom one redundant. This change simplifies the clock management logic by eliminating duplicate state tracking.

The commit focuses on refactoring code related to RISC-V memory management, specifically addressing the use of #ifdef CONFIG_XIP_KERNEL preprocessor directives. It aims to reduce the usage of these directives by grouping related code and moving macros closer to their declarations. This improves code organization and readability without changing functional behavior.

This commit enables QUP (Qualcomm Universal Peripheral) and GPI (General Purpose Interface) DMA hardware support in the device tree for the sm6350-lena platform. The purpose is to prepare the hardware infrastructure to allow for functioning I2C nodes to be added later. It modifies the device tree source (DTS) file to enable specific hardware blocks that are required for I2C communication.

The commit fixes comments in the thermal driver for rzg2l, specifically replacing an unclear comment about 'Capture times' with 'Total number of ADC data samples', correcting a typo from 'caliberation' to 'calibration', and ensuring that comment blocks follow proper coding style by placing '/*' on a separate line. These are all purely documentation and style changes without altering any functional behavior.

This commit fixes an infinite loop in the igc driver's release_swfw_sync function. The issue occurs when failing to acquire the HW semaphore during resource release, which typically happens when the hardware is surprise-removed. The fix logs an error and exits gracefully instead of looping infinitely. The change is a targeted bug fix that addresses a specific failure condition in hardware resource management.

The commit updates the pcm3168a ASoC driver to use modern terminology for clocking instead of the older defines for bus clocks. It is part of a larger effort to remove old-style definitions and adopt more modern ASoC DAI format conventions. The change involves updating the driver's clocking interface to align with current kernel standards.

This commit removes the IS_SWAPFILE hack from the NFS codebase. The commit message clearly states that the code is pointless because IS_SWAPFILE is always defined, so the conditional logic is redundant and can be safely removed. This is a straightforward cleanup change that eliminates unnecessary code.

This commit implements GTP echo response functionality in the Linux kernel's GTP (GPRS Tunneling Protocol) subsystem. It addresses a specific issue where GTP-U instances created via 'ip link' lack a userspace daemon to handle GTP messages, particularly Echo Requests. The commit ensures that when a GTP packet with GTP_ECHO_REQ message type arrives, a GTP_ECHO_RSP is sent back to the sender. The response includes an information element with the GTPIE_RECOVERY tag and restart counter value, with different handling for GTPv0 (counter = 0) and GTPv1 (counter from userspace via IFLA_GTP_RESTART_COUNT). This is a targeted implementation of a missing protocol behavior to comply with GTP specifications.

The commit replaces cpumask_next_* functions with cpumask_first_* functions where appropriate, specifically when n == -1 (which means start == 0). It also introduces a new cpumask_first_zero() function. The change improves performance by using a more efficient function and enhances code clarity by using the appropriate function for the given use case.

The commit removes a call to the 'dtim_assoc' IOVAR in the brcmfmac driver. This change addresses an issue where the host driver was attempting to retrieve TIM (Traffic Indication Map) information from the firmware after a station (STA) roamed between access points. The firmware no longer supports this functionality, causing repeated error messages on the console. The fix ensures that the host driver no longer attempts this unsupported operation.

This commit adds support for a new board configuration (adl_rt5682) in the Intel SOF driver for the rt5682 codec. It specifically enables support for a headset codec (alc5682) without a speaker amplifier by connecting it to SSP0, following Intel's BT offload design. The change is focused on hardware configuration and driver support for a specific platform setup.

This commit ensures that zerocopy sendmsg operations fail when the underlying socket protocol does not support zerocopy. It extends a previous patch that already handled zerocopy send requests for unsupported protocols, applying the same logic to sendmsg operations. The change involves modifying the io_uring net subsystem to check protocol support before attempting zerocopy sends, preventing potential errors or undefined behavior.

This commit addresses an issue in the documentation build process where xelatex fails when trying to load the xeCJK.sty package on systems where the texlive-xecjk package is not properly installed or available. The fix involves conditionally checking for the existence of xeCJK.sty before attempting to load it, which prevents build errors on certain distributions like RHEL 9 and its clones. The change is specifically targeted at improving the kerneldoc preamble handling for PDF documentation generation.

This commit modifies the checkpatch.pl script to allow 'case' macros without requiring them to be enclosed in parentheses. The change addresses a specific error message that was being triggered by legitimate kernel driver code using such macros. The commit updates the script's logic to recognize and permit these patterns, which are already in use by some in-kernel drivers.

The commit converts the drm/simpledrm driver from using simple-KMS helpers to regular atomic helpers. This involves replacing the older helper functions with more modern and better-architected atomic helpers, which improve code sharing among drivers. The changes remove empty CRTC helpers and unconditionally run drm_atomic_helper_check_plane_state(). No functional changes are introduced.

This commit adds a JPEG decode worker interface to the MediaTek JPEG decoder driver (mtk-jpegdec). The purpose is to enable parallel execution of three hardware units on the MT8195 chip, improving performance by utilizing multiple hardware engines simultaneously. The change involves adding new code to manage worker threads for JPEG decoding tasks.

The commit removes unnecessary VPRINTK() calls from the libata library and also removes the ata_scsi_dump_cdb() function. The rationale is that the information previously provided by these calls is already covered by tracepoints, making them redundant. The removal simplifies the code and avoids duplication of information that can be retrieved from existing SCSI tracing mechanisms.

This commit adds support for zstate (a low-power display state) during extended vblank periods when entering FREESYNC_STATE_VIDEO. It modifies the behavior during FREESYNC transitions to utilize extra vblank time for entering zstate if sufficient time is available (more than 5ms). The changes involve triggering full updates, calculating time gains, and adjusting register values for min_dst_y_next_start. The commit is focused on optimizing power consumption and display behavior during FREESYNC operation.

The commit fixes a type mismatch in the hqd_destroy() callback function. Specifically, it corrects the type of the reset_type parameter from 'enum kfd_preempt_type' to 'uint32_t' to align with the actual function pointer prototype. This resolves a Clang Control Flow Integrity (CFI) failure that occurs when booting a kernel with CONFIG_CFI_CLANG on RX 6700 XT hardware.

The commit corrects the property name for reset bindings in device tree bindings for STMicroelectronics STM32 pinctrl drivers. It changes 'reset' to 'resets' and adds 'additionalProperties: false' to prevent future issues. This is a precise fix for a device tree binding specification error.

The commit addresses a compiler warning (array-bounds) in the ath9k wireless driver. It silences a specific GCC 12 warning by reclassifying the warning level from default to W=1+, which suppresses the array bounds check. The change is minimal and targeted, affecting only the warning level classification in the build system.

The commit modifies the crypto driver for the sun8i-ss hardware to use pm_runtime_resume_and_get instead of pm_runtime_get_sync. This change simplifies the code by using a more appropriate runtime power management function. The commit message indicates this is a code simplification effort, and the change is related to power management in a crypto driver.

This commit refactors the io_uring subsystem by splitting out fixed file installation and removal functionality into the filetable code section. It also modifies helper functions to accept a context (ctx) instead of an io_kiocb, as the operation is not request-specific but rather related to the ring itself. The change improves code organization and clarity by placing related functionality together.

This commit splits the G1 and G2 decoder nodes in the device tree bindings for the NXP i.MX8MQ VPU. The G1 and G2 are independent decoder blocks that were previously represented as a single node, but now have separate nodes because they are controlled by the same power-domain controller (vpu-blk-ctrl). The change updates the device tree bindings to reflect this hardware independence and proper power domain support. The commit also notes that the new device tree configuration with the old kernel is not supported.

The commit removes the IWL_DEVICE_BZ_COMMON macro from the iwlwifi driver code, as it is only used once within the IWL_DEVICE_BZ macro. The change involves combining the contents of the macro directly into the single location where it's used, simplifying the code structure and eliminating an unnecessary abstraction.
